title: Task: Critique activation options for a Kernel `philosophy.md` from a maintenance and misuse perspective. Read: - /Users/masayuki/AGENTS.md - /Users/masayuki/.claude/CODEX.md - /Users/masayuki/.codex/prompts/kernel.md Deliver: - Compare 3 activation patterns: (A) always load in CODEX.md, (B) load on...
summary_text: **Scoring basis**: `Safety` = misuse resistance, `Usefulness` = decision quality lift, `Complexity` = operational/maintenance burden (`10` = highest burden). | Pattern | Safety | Usefulness | Complexity | Maintenance + misuse critique | |---|---:|---:|---:|---| | A) Always load in `CODEX.md` | 5 | 6 | 4 | Easy to wire, but violates the “keep always-loaded files thin” direction and increases global side-effects. Higher misuse blast radius because philosophy text influences every run, even when irrelevant. | | B) Load only via explicit mention in `kernel`/`vote` prompts | 8 | 8 | 5 | Best alignment with command-source-of-truth in prompt contracts. Scoped activation lowers accidental overreach and keeps adapter files lean. Main risk is drift between `kernel.md` and `vote.md`, which is controllable. | | C) Conditional orchestrator load when values/tradeoffs matter | 7 | 7 | 8 | Potentially elegant, but highest ambiguity and policy drift risk. Hard to audit “when values matter,” easier to game/mis-trigger, and harder to test deterministically. | Best pattern: **B (explicit prompt-level activation)**. Guardrails: 1. Keep `philosophy.md` principles-only; no procedural/runtime contract duplication. 2. Add the same explicit load hook in both `kernel.md` and `vote.md` (and parity-check with `k.md`/`v.md`). 3. Define precedence: `AGENTS.md`/`CODEX.md`/command contracts override `philosophy.md` on conflict. 4. Add a lightweight check (CI/script) that prompt files still reference `philosophy.md`. 5. Log when philosophy was loaded, so misuse/debugging is auditable.
